steps.title: Recognize equal angles create similar triangles
steps.content:
The figure marks $\angle XWQ = a°$ and $\angle ZYQ = a°$ (equal angles at opposite vertices).

Since $WZ$ and $XY$ intersect at $Q$, the angles $\angle WQX$ and $\angle YQZ$ are vertical angles and therefore equal.

By AA (Angle-Angle) similarity, triangle $WXQ \sim$ triangle $YZQ$, with correspondence $W \leftrightarrow Y$, $X \leftrightarrow Z$.

steps.title: Set up proportion of corresponding sides
steps.content:
For similar triangles, corresponding sides are proportional:
$$\frac{WQ}{YQ} = \frac{WX}{YZ}$$
Substitute the known values:

steps.title: Solve for YZ
steps.content:
$$\frac{70}{21} = \frac{60}{YZ}$$
Cross-multiply:
$$70 \cdot YZ = 60 \cdot 21$$
$$70 \cdot YZ = 1260$$
$$YZ = \frac{1260}{70} = 18$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
